question: Do common open chord voicings (C, A, G, E, D) share structural patterns that predict their moveable barre chord forms — i.e., is the CAGED system derivable from observation of open chord geometry rather than being an arbitrary pedagogical framework?
hypothesis: The five open chord shapes share a structural property — each preserves its intervallic relationships when transposed via barre, and the five shapes tile the entire fretboard as a mathematical consequence of standard tuning's interval pattern (P4-P4-P4-M3-P4).
evidence: Each open shape contains only Root, M3, P5 in various voicings — sliding preserves intervals because string-to-string intervals are fixed by tuning
Standard tuning's P4-P4-P4-M3-P4 pattern creates exactly 5 distinct shape regions that interlock cyclically
The CAGED ordering is one of 5 cyclic permutations — the name is mnemonic, the 5-shape cycle is structural
Altering the tuning (all-4ths, open G, drop D) produces different shape systems, confirming CAGED is tuning-derived
Cross-validated with GPT-4o: high agreement on all 5 analysis points (geometric structure, barre transposition, ordering, tuning dependence, discovery vs invention)
